title: Q
wikitext:
[]: Q             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 8:30 am Is there a reason Evangelion 3.0 is titled Q instead of Kyu ? Well, yes, there's the whole "quickening" thing, but I'm going to disregard that so this post has a purpose for being. And really, I'm getting ahead of where I should be, but only because even if Khara never thought of or considered this, it would be really awesome if they did . In terms of Biblical study, "Q" refers to the Q source, a hypothetical lost document that, along with Mark (Spoilers in link!), inspired the Gospels of Matthew and Luke. A substantial portion of Biblical scholars believe Matthew and Luke were based on the Gospel of Mark, but these two contain information in common that adds to and in some cases contradicts Mark. Thus is postulated the existence of another common source, called "Q" from the German word "Quelle". So yeah, "Q source" is redundant. Q is significant if it existed because it contained all the quotes attributed to Jesus unless it didn't exist in which case it did not. What does this mean for Evangelion: Q ? In all likelihood, absolutely nothing. This thread should probably be locked.

[]: Re: Q             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Sun Apr 18, 2010 8:36 am NAveryW wrote: Is there a reason Evangelion 3.0 is titled Q instead of Kyu ? Well, yes, there's the whole "quickening" thing, but I'm going to disregard that so this post has a purpose for being. And really, I'm getting ahead of where I should be, but only because even if Khara never thought of or considered this, it would be really awesome if they did . In terms of Biblical study, "Q" refers to the Q source, a hypothetical lost document that, along with Mark (Spoilers in link!), inspired the Gospels of Matthew and Luke. A substantial portion of Biblical scholars believe Matthew and Luke were based on the Gospel of Mark, but these two contain information in common that adds to and in some cases contradicts Mark. Thus is postulated the existence of another common source, called "Q" from the German word "Quelle". So yeah, "Q source" is redundant. Q is significant if it existed because it contained all the quotes attributed to Jesus unless it didn't exist in which case it did not. What does this mean for Evangelion: Q ? In all likelihood, absolutely nothing. This thread should probably be locked.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:26 am SSD's own two cents: Jyo-Ha-Kyu (Prolouge, Destroy, Rush) from Noh plays. They simply swapped out "Kyu" for "Q" to maintain a play on words (Kyu/Q) and they went with "Quickening"* as the full title. *This term means fetal movement (Shinji and Rei inside Yui/Eva-01. They're back in the womb, so to speak.) but, my guess is it could also be a quick, fast, story; one event after another.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:30 am THERE WEREN'T SUPPOSED TO BE SPOILERS IN THIS THREAD AND THE TOPIC WAS ONLY SUPPOSED TO BE ONE LETTER LONG! CHANGE IT BACK!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:38 am Not gonna change it back until 2.22 is out, at the very least. Wait till May 26 at the earliest.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:40 am UrsusArctos wrote: Not gonna change it back until 2.22 is out, at the very least. Wait till May 26 at the earliest.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:45 am Maybe not that post, but later posts? If you remove that word from the title, all spoilers will have to be hidden within spoiler tags and obnoxious spoiler rules will apply in this thread. At present, you can have all the spoiler-filled discussion you want about what might happen in 3.0/ Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:45 am People typically aren't going to know about the change from Kyu to Q (Quickening) unless they saw the 2.0 film, so the title will be a spoiler in itself. (If you want a thread to be spoiler-free, you have to mark it "No spoilers" otherwise the staff will have to make a judgment call.)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:48 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: People typically aren't going to know about the change from Kyu to Q (Quickening) unless they saw the 2.0 film, so the title will be a spoiler in itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 18, 2010 11:52 am The full title is Quickening, the preview even displays such. (Hence the title being considered a spoiler.)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 12:02 pm And when all the movies are out, people who haven't seen any of the films yet will be buying a 4-disc set that clearly has the title of the third film on the package. The whole thread has now been about the title being a spoiler. It was supposed to be about something else equally pointless. I don't like myself anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 18, 2010 12:08 pm If this thread was meant to be about something else, you have to show that in the thread title somewhere. Same with marking this as spoilers/no spoilers. Otherwise how will people know what it's actually about? They're going to think this is film-title discussion/speculation/theories like I did.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 18, 2010 12:19 pm Right. Q by itself means nothing, but when attached to "Kyu", it runs into a huge tornado marked "SPOILER TERRITORY". Like I said, it's far more sense to make it a spoiler thread than a no-spoiler one. Obnoxious spoiler rules remain in effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Sun Apr 18, 2010 12:39 pm Maybe the Q is a reference to the 1965 series Ultra Q. Just a thought since like NGE it tries to be heavy in mystery and whatnot.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 18, 2010 2:16 pm Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion wrote: Maybe the Q is a reference to the 1965 series Ultra Q.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun Apr 18, 2010 6:06 pm Yeah not really fond of the title "Q". I like things to be uniform. 3.0 would have been just fine. Interesting theories on it though. Don't have much to add. Maybe the Q is a reference to the 1965 series Ultra Q. Just a thought since like NGE it tries to be heavy in mystery and whatnot.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 18, 2010 8:02 pm skikes wrote: Yeah not really fond of the title "Q". I like things to be uniform. 3.0 would have been just fine.

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Sun Apr 18, 2010 10:50 pm 序 - beginning 破 - rising action 急/Q - climax (quickining) ? - ending

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 19, 2010 11:00 am I split out some of the spammy posts. Stay on topic, dorks. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Apr 19, 2010 3:56 pm Q is actually a fairly common way to represent the kanji 急. The nickname for the Fuji-Express(Kyuko) (富士急行) is the Fuji-Q (富士Q)
